metadata.task_description: 为 AI-VIDEO 项目规划前端方案。AI-VIDEO 是纯本地 Python CLI，通过 YAML 配置驱动 ComfyUI 生成视频链。核心流程：读取配置 → 渲染 workflow JSON → 提交 ComfyUI → 提取帧 → ffmpeg 拼接。需要前端来改善：shot list 可视化编辑、生成结果预览、长时间生成监控、历史 runs 浏览、参数调优体验。

architecture_patterns: CLI-only Python application (no existing web layer)
YAML-driven configuration with Pydantic v2 validation
Binding-driven workflow rendering (WorkflowBinding maps JSON paths)
Sequential shot chaining via last-frame extraction
Dependency injection in PipelineRunner (comfy/ffmpeg as injected deps)

error_handling.pattern: typed AiVideoError with ErrorCode enum, retryable flag, user_message + technical_detail
error_handling.error_codes: 12 distinct ErrorCode values covering config, workflow, ComfyUI, ffmpeg, manifest, disk

coding_conventions.data_models: Pydantic v2 BaseModel for all config/manifest models
coding_conventions.testing: pytest with mocked dependencies (httpx.MockTransport, FakeComfy, FakeFfmpeg)

tech_stack.build_system: setuptools with pyproject.toml, local editable install

current_limitations: No web server or API layer exists
No WebSocket support (polling only for ComfyUI job status)
No existing frontend code, assets, or build tooling
Config editing requires manual YAML editing
Run monitoring requires checking CLI output or manifest.json files manually

source_code.notes: All Pydantic models that a frontend API must expose and validate. Key models for UI: ProjectConfig (project settings), ShotSpec/ShotList (shot editor), CharacterProfile (character management), WorkflowBinding (binding visualization)
ComfyUI HTTP API surface that frontend needs to understand or proxy: /system_stats, /upload/image, /prompt, /queue, /history/{prompt_id}, /view, /free. JobStatus enum is critical for monitoring UI.
Core orchestration logic. run() and resume() are the main entry points. Frontend needs to trigger these and monitor progress. Currently synchronous and blocking.
Pure function for rendering workflow JSON. Frontend could use preview/validation of workflow before submission.
Run state persistence. Frontend run browser needs to read manifests. ShotRecord/RunManifest are the primary data structures for monitoring UI.
YAML loading and validation. Frontend needs equivalent functionality for config editor. Path resolution logic is important.
Current user-facing entry point. Frontend will replace or supplement CLI commands. validate/run/resume commands map to frontend actions.
Loads and converts workflow JSON templates. UI workflow converter is useful for preview in frontend.
ffmpeg subprocess calls. Frontend preview needs to understand clip metadata from probe_clip(). Stitching triggers are frontend actions.
Error model that frontend must present to users. ErrorCode enum maps to UI error states. retryable flag determines UI retry options.

config.notes: No web dependencies exist. Frontend will need new dependencies (FastAPI/Flask for API server, or separate JS frontend build)
Example project config structure that UI editor must support
Example shot list structure that UI editor must support
Real Wan 2.2 project config (no characters, higher resolution)
Binding structure with character_refs. Frontend binding editor must understand JsonPath bindings.

run_artifacts.notes: Real completed run manifest with 3 shots, status tracking, artifact hashes. Template for frontend run browser.
Empty/stuck run manifest. Frontend must handle incomplete runs gracefully.

external.usage: HTTP client for ComfyUI REST API (synchronous Client, not AsyncClient)
Data models, validation, serialization for all config and manifest structures
Loading project config and shot list YAML files

comfyui_api_endpoints.usage: Submit rendered workflow JSON for execution
Check queue status for running/pending jobs
Get job execution history and outputs

breaking_changes: Adding an API server layer requires new async patterns (current code is synchronous)
Pipeline.run() is blocking; frontend needs non-blocking execution
ComfyClient uses synchronous httpx.Client; WebSocket/async support needed for live monitoring
No existing CORS/authentication; adding web server introduces security surface
Config editing currently file-based; web editor needs write-back with validation

affected_modules: comfy_client (needs WebSocket or SSE for live progress)
manifest (needs concurrent-safe reads from web server)
cli (may need to coexist with web server entry point)
conflict_detection.mitigation_strategy: Incremental layering: add a thin API server (FastAPI) that wraps existing sync code with run-in-executor, preserving CLI as primary interface. WebSocket progress via ComfyUI's native WS as Phase 2 after basic API works. Config editor uses read-validate-write pattern against existing Pydantic models rather than replacing them.

integration_points.description: Must be made non-blocking for web server. Options: asyncio.to_thread, background task queue, or refactor to async.
Blocking poll loop with time.sleep(). Frontend needs SSE or WebSocket stream of progress events.
atomic_write_manifest uses file replace. Web server must handle concurrent reads during writes.
Read-only currently. Frontend editor needs save functionality with Pydantic re-validation.
No index of runs. Frontend run browser needs efficient manifest discovery across runs/.

shot_list_editor.current_gap: Manual YAML editing only; no validation UI; no visual ordering

generation_monitoring.current_gap: Blocking CLI output only; no real-time progress; no cancel support; ComfyUI WebSocket not yet integrated
generation_monitoring.comfyui_websocket_potential: ComfyUI natively supports WebSocket at ws://host/ws?clientId=... for execution_start, progress, executing, execution_cached, execution_error events

result_preview.current_gap: Must browse filesystem manually; no in-browser video playback; last_frame.png not easily accessible

run_history.current_gap: No run index; must scan runs/ directory; no run comparison; no cleanup UI

parameter_tuning.current_gap: Edit YAML → validate → run cycle is slow; no instant feedback on parameter changes; no workflow JSON preview
